what_it_does: Implements a one-pass, streaming backfill driver that bulk-ingests historical conversation JSONL files from configured capture-source directories into the recollection database. It reuses existing capture primitives (source registry, file discovery, parsing, batch embedding via :8007, and DB insertion) and advances a per-archive high-water mark so reruns are idempotent and resumable.
observations: Uses the capture-source registry (CAPTURE_SOURCES / getCaptureSourceDirs) instead of hardcoded paths, including agent-*.jsonl sidechains via findJsonlFiles.
Streams one file at a time rather than collecting all unprocessed conversations in memory.
Deterministic exchange ids are based on archivePath:lineStart-lineEnd, and writes use INSERT OR REPLACE.
Per-archive high-water mark (MAX(line_end)) is stored in the DB, making the DB itself the checkpoint.
Copies the source file into the archive dir when no archive exists or when any lines have already been indexed, to refresh potentially grown sources.
Batch-embeds slices of new exchanges via generateExchangeEmbeddingsBatch, then inserts each exchange with its vector.
Honors a maxConvs cap and logs a checkpoint trail per ingested conversation.
Fails loudly on embedding/parse/insert errors; surfaces per-file errors in the result and log.
Only writes to recollection's own isolated store and reads source transcripts read-only.
standouts: Idempotency with live sync is explicitly load-bearing: the identical archive-path convention makes backfill and live-sync exchange ids match by construction.
The DB is the checkpoint — no separate checkpoint file — with line-level resume via the per-archive high-water mark.
ingestFileIncremental is extracted as a shared per-file unit usable by both backfill and potential live-cleanup paths.
Batch embedding is followed by an explicit length invariant check to refuse vector mis-pairing.
conventions: Heavy rationale comments and section references (IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE).
Fail-loud error handling: no silent fallback, no fabricated vectors.
Uses .js extension in TypeScript imports for ESM compatibility.
Constants are UPPER_SNAKE_CASE; default batch size is a named constant.
Result type is an honest tally with explicit failure collection.
Read-only source access and isolated write paths are documented as safety invariants.
concerns: Copies the entire source file to archive on every resume (maxIndexedLine > 0), which could be expensive for large or numerous files.
Serial file processing with no concurrency may make a ~13k-conversation backfill very slow.
insertExchange is called once per exchange; if it commits a transaction per call, batch throughput may be low.
If a source file shrinks below the stored high-water mark, exchanges beyond the new end will be silently skipped.
The resume gate uses lineStart > maxIndexedLine, which assumes line ordering and no overlapping ranges.
Re-embeds exchanges on every rerun rather than reusing previously computed vectors.
errors array stores only message strings, not stack traces or error types.
No explicit validation that generateExchangeEmbeddingsBatch vector dimensions match the schema.
notes: This is the bulk head-start driver: it turns the existing incremental capture primitives into a resumable, idempotent one-pass ingestion over the historical corpus, including sidechain transcripts.
questions: What is the exact contract and failure mode of insertExchange (transaction scope, vector dimension validation)?
How does parseConversation assign lineStart/lineEnd for exchanges, especially across sidechain files?
What does the :8007 embedding lens expect in generateExchangeEmbeddingsBatch and how does it report errors?
How are excluded projects formatted and where are they configured?
Is there a CLI-level reentrancy guard or single-instance lock for backfillConversations?
How does the live sync path ensure it never races with backfill on the same file?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is explicitly a streaming, resumable backfill driver that reuses live-capture primitives and is idempotent with live sync.
intention_signals: Provide a safe, one-time bulk ingestion of the existing conversation corpus that does not fork the capture pipeline, can be interrupted and resumed, and coexists with ongoing live sync without double-inserting.
novelty: The combination of deterministic exchange ids keyed on archive path plus line ranges, per-archive line-level high-water marks stored in the same DB table, and identical archive-path conventions makes backfill and live sync mutually idempotent without a separate checkpoint mechanism.
contribution: A reusable per-file incremental ingestion primitive (ingestFileIncremental) and a driver (backfillConversations) that bulk-imports historical conversations while preserving the same identity scheme, embedding path, and DB write path used by live capture, giving downstream data-dependent beats a real corpus to work with.
summary_for_embedding: Backfill driver that streams capture-source JSONL conversation files into the recollection database using batch embeddings and per-archive high-water-mark checkpointing.
intention_for_embedding: To bulk-ingest the historical conversation corpus once, reusing live-capture primitives so backfill and incremental sync are idempotent and resume-safe.

symbols.does: Describes the outcome of a backfill run, including counts of scanned, ingested, skipped, and failed files plus captured exchanges and per-file errors.
symbols.description: A plain data interface used as the return type for backfillConversations. It exposes an honest, no-silent-failures tally and an errors array so callers can inspect every failure.

symbols.does: Configuration object for backfillConversations, including conversation cap, embedding batch size, project filter, optional DB handle, and optional log sink.
symbols.description: Provides knobs for bounded verification runs (maxConvs), batch throughput (batchSize), staged debugging (onlyProject), and test/verify reuse (db, log).

symbols.does: Ingests a single conversation file incrementally by high-water mark: copies to archive if needed, parses, filters new exchanges, batch-embeds them, and inserts each with its vector.
symbols.description: The shared per-file ingestion unit. It uses the same archive-path convention as live sync for deterministic exchange ids, refreshes the archive copy when the source may have grown, and throws on parse/embed/insert failures so callers surface rather than swallow errors.

symbols.does: Drives the full backfill over all enabled capture-source directories, streaming files one at a time, honoring a conversation cap, and logging progress.
symbols.description: The top-level driver. It initializes the embedding lens, opens or borrows a DB handle, walks capture sources and projects, calls ingestFileIncremental per file, tallies results, and ensures the DB is closed only if it opened it.
